user.title: Connect PPC Smart Meter Gateway (by Lutarym)
user.description:
Enter the IP address or hostname of your gateway. It will be checked for reachability on port 443 before you can enter login credentials.

Installed integration version: {version}

credentials.title: Gateway login

meters.title: Select meters

tariffs.title: Select evaluation profiles

history.title: Optional: import historical data

error.invalid_auth: Login rejected by the gateway (HTTP 401) - username or password is incorrect.
error.parsing_error: Login was successful (credentials are correct!), but the gateway's response page could not be read. See details below.
error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the gateway on port 443. Check the IP address and network route.
error.no_meters_found: No meters were found on the gateway. See details below.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the gateway.

import_history.name: Import history
import_history.description: One-time import of a corrected historical statistics series for the 1-0:1.8.0 (grid draw) sensor. Two modes: csv_path (1:1 import of a TraveNetz export, takes priority) or start_date+source_entity (scaled interpolation between a start anchor and the current live value).

csv_path.name: CSV file path (1:1 import)
csv_path.description: Path to a TraveNetz export file already present on the HA host, e.g. /config/imsys_export.csv. Imported 1:1 - no scaling, no other entity. Takes priority over start_date/source_entity if both are given.

start_date.name: Start date (scaled mode only)
start_date.description: The date the historical series should start at. Only used when csv_path is not set.

start_value.name: Start value (kWh)
start_value.description: The known correct cumulative value at the start (start_date for scaled mode, or the CSV's first row for csv_path mode - 0 if it starts at meter installation).

source_entity.name: Source entity (scaled mode only)
source_entity.description: Entity whose statistics provide the day-to-day shape for interpolation (e.g. a less accurate but gapless alternative meter). Where this entity itself has no data (e.g. before it was installed), that portion is interpolated evenly over time. Only used when csv_path is not set.

target_entity.name: Target entity (optional)
target_entity.description: The 1-0:1.8.0 sensor to write to. Auto-detected if left empty - only works automatically when exactly one gateway is configured.

monthly_kwh.name: Known monthly values (scaled mode only, optional)
monthly_kwh.description: Map of "YYYY-MM": kWh for months where the exact consumption is already known - takes priority over the source entity's estimate for that month. Example: {"2026-01": 130.5, "2026-03": 95}

dry_run.name: Dry run
dry_run.description: If enabled, only computes and returns a summary (incl. monthly breakdown) without writing anything to the statistics.

repair_statistics_reset.name: Repair statistics reset
repair_statistics_reset.description: Precisely corrects a statistics reset (sum dropped to 0 while state kept updating correctly, e.g. after a connection outage) - no estimation, just shifts the already-recorded values from the given point onward by the correct offset (last valid value before it). Recommended: disable the recorder first, re-enable afterwards.

since.name: Since
since.description: The point in time the reset occurred (e.g. the first hour with sum=0 in Developer Tools → Actions → recorder.get_statistics).

target_entity.name: Target entity (optional)
target_entity.description: The 1-0:1.8.0 sensor to repair. Auto-detected if left empty - only works automatically when exactly one gateway is configured.

dry_run.name: Dry run
dry_run.description: If enabled, only computes and returns a summary without writing anything to the statistics.

repair_erroneous_ramp.name: Repair erroneous ramp
repair_erroneous_ramp.description: Removes an erroneously inserted linear ramp (counterpart to 'Repair statistics reset' - here the excess is too HIGH instead of too LOW, typically from a bad bridge with a wrong target value). The ramp period is flattened, everything after it is corrected downward by the excess. Recommended: disable the recorder first, re-enable afterwards.

ramp_start.name: Ramp start
ramp_start.description: The point in time the erroneous ramp begins (last good value is before this).

ramp_end.name: Ramp end
ramp_end.description: The point in time values return to normal (small, plausible increments).

target_entity.name: Target entity (optional)
target_entity.description: The 1-0:1.8.0 sensor to repair. Auto-detected if left empty - only works automatically when exactly one gateway is configured.

dry_run.name: Dry run
dry_run.description: If enabled, only computes and returns a summary without writing anything to the statistics.
